title: Phyllostegia mannii
intro: Phyllostegia mannii, also known as Hawaiian hedge nettle or island hedge nettle, is a flowering plant native to the Hawaiian Islands. The plant has been traditionally used in Hawaiian medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Phyllostegia mannii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Phyllostegia mannii is often prepared as a tincture or infused in hot water as a tea to support immune function and alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections.
taxonomy: Phyllostegia mannii is a species of flowering plant that belongs to the family Lamiaceae, commonly known as the mint family. This family is further divided into several subfamilies, including Lamioideae, which Phyllostegia mannii falls under. As a member of the Lamiaceae family, Phyllostegia mannii is closely related to other plants such as lavender, rosemary, and thyme. The genus Phyllostegia itself is native to Hawaii and consists of several species of flowering plants. Phyllostegia mannii is a rare and endemic species found only in the Hawaiian Islands.
